title: Mildly Crispy Italian Turkey Piadina
description: A savory, mildly crispy piadina filled with sautéed turkey mince, spinach, and provolone cheese, cooked in a traditional molcajete for a quick lunch or dinner.
instructions: In a bowl, whisk flour, salt, and pepper together.
Add warm water and olive oil to the dry mix, stirring until a sticky dough forms.
Knead the dough on a lightly floured surface for 5 minutes until smooth, then cover and let rest for 20 minutes.
While the dough rests, heat a skillet over medium heat and sauté the diced onion until translucent, about 3 minutes.
Add minced garlic to the pan, cook for 30 seconds, then stir in the turkey mince, cooking until browned, about 8 minutes.
Season the turkey mixture with oregano and a pinch of pepper, then let it simmer for 2 minutes, stirring.
Turn off the heat and spread a thin layer of tomato sauce over each rolled-out dough rectangle.
Sprinkle the cooked turkey mixture evenly over the sauce, top with spinach slices and provolone cheese.
Fold the edges of the dough over the filling and press to seal, forming a pocket.
Place the prepared piadina on a hot molcajete or griddle, cooking each side for 3 minutes until golden and crisp.
Remove from heat, let cool slightly, then slice into wedges and serve immediately.
